text:
Once upon a moonlit night, there lived a rabbit named Thumper. Thumper couldn't quite believe his good fortune. Every morning, he'd hop past sleepy squirrels and chirp cheerfully at nightbirds. Yet nothing awaited him farther than his favorite burrow.

One day, dreaming big dreams, Thumper decided he wanted to fly. Yes, fly! He spent countless hours soaring on balloons fashioned from moss. Each draft inspired him; he imagined crossing oceans. Then one breezy afternoon, his balloon soared too high, tangled with oak leaves.

Thumper fluttered down gently onto an old oak. Birds laughed as they glanced upward. He got back to his leaps and hops. Life had ways of teaching. Next dawn, a hawk swooped down. Thumper saw only stars.

"Oh dear," he thought sadly.

Then he heard a voice.

"I never thought starlings could sing."

It was Old Owl, singing as he dusted feathers. "You should learn," he chuckled
